title: polypodium alavae
intro: Polypodium alavae, also known as Alava's polypody, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, native to Mexico. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to have various medicinal applications. The plant is believed to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Polypodium alavae are typically made by drying and powdering the leaves, which can be used to make teas, capsules, or infusions to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
